title: Lemonade Q1 2026 revenue jumps as losses narrow
text: Lemonade Q1 2026: revenue jumps as losses narrow. Revenue $258 million, up 71% year-over-year. In-force premium $1.33 billion, up 32% year-over-year. Gross profit $100 million, up 159%. Customer growth 158,000 new customers, 37% increase. Adjusted free cash flow $17 million positive fourth consecutive quarter. Net loss $36 million ($0.47 per share) vs $62 million prior year. Adjusted EBITDA loss $17 million, 64% improvement year-over-year. In-force premium per employee exceeded $1 million. Annual dollar retention 85% unchanged sequentially. Gross loss ratio 62% with 5% catastrophe losses. Loss adjustment expense ratio 6%. CEO Daniel Schreiber emphasized: Since Q1 2023, grown spend roughly 200% maintaining LTV CAC ratio above 3. Ten consecutive quarters accelerating growth. Raised full-year 2026 guidance expecting positive EBITDA Q4 2026, throughout 2027. Pet insurance milestone $500 million in-force premium. Autonomous vehicle insurance 70% higher conversion rates pilot markets. 18% total in-force premium bundled multiple product lines.
